codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly adds all three required manifest fields to package.json: (1) 'main' field pointing to './extension.js', (2) non-empty 'contributes.commands' array with 4 commands matching the command registrations in extension.js, and (3) activationEvents correctly covering .ui.md files.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tooling-worker skill procedure for VS Code extension work was followed correctly. Worker read package.json and extension.js to understand the command registrations before making edits. No deviations from the documented procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker read both package.json and extension.js, identified the 4 commands registered (uiMd.validate, uiMd.showPreview, uiMd.insertScreenTemplate, uiMd.insertStateTemplate), then added corresponding contributes.commands entries

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new conventions were established or violated. The feature correctly addressed a manifest-only fix with no broader architectural implications.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No AGENTS.md or services.yaml gaps identified

summary: Review PASSED. The vscode-extension-manifest-fix feature correctly implements all three expected behaviors: (1) package.json has valid 'main' field set to './extension.js', (2) package.json has non-empty 'contributes.commands' array with 4 command declarations matching extension.js registrations, (3) activationEvents already correctly cover .ui.md files via 'onLanguage:ui-md' and 'workspaceContains:**/*.ui.md'. The diff shows 19 insertions to package.json with no deletions. Worker followed the tooling-worker skill procedure correctly. No blocking issues found.
